```xml
Operation MidnightEclipse: Cyber Attack on Water and Wastewater Systems
Water and Wastewater Systems Complex A sophisticated multi-stage attack targeting critical infrastructure, leveraging a zero-day vulnerability in Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS to disrupt water treatment operations.
name=Operation MidnightEclipse Sabotage critical infrastructure to cause environmental harm or service disruption Basic
Water and Wastewater Systems Internet-facing assets Vulnerability exploit
AVAILABLE_SURFACES: ["Internet-facing assets"]
AVAILABLE_VECTORS: ["Vulnerability exploit"]
Network Scanning Internet-facing assets Open port N/A CAPEC-309 Adversary scans open ports and identifies outdated firmware on SCADA systems to map network topology and identify potential targets.
Compromised Legitimate Infrastructure Shared DB or directory Security misconfigurations and vulnerabilities N/A CAPEC-544 Threat actors compromise servers hosting ICS management tools to create a foothold near the target network.
Exploit Public-Facing Vulnerability (CVE-2024-3400) Internet-facing assets Vulnerability exploit CVE-2017-6627 (Suggested): Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS GlobalProtect VPN Remote Code Execution CAPEC-184 Attackers exploit a zero-day vulnerability in Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS GlobalProtect VPN to achieve remote code execution on exposed systems.
Command and Scripting Interpreter Obsolescent products Malware CVE-2022-42827 (Suggested): Microsoft Exchange Server Remote Code Execution CAPEC-160 Malicious PowerShell scripts are executed to deploy backdoors and disable security features on infected hosts.
Scheduled Task/Job Weak passwords Brute-force attack N/A CAPEC-654 Recurring scheduled tasks are created to maintain persistent access and re-execute payloads post-reboot.
Exploiting Weak Credentials Weak passwords Compromised and weak credentials N/A CAPEC-600 Default or weak admin credentials for ICS controllers are guessed using brute-force attacks to escalate privileges.
Indicator Removal Shared DB or directory Inadequate encryption N/A CAPEC-506 Logs from ICS firewalls and SIEM systems are deleted to obscure the attack timeline and evade detection.
Dumping Passwords Improper configuration Browser-based attack N/A CAPEC-406 Plaintext credentials are extracted from memory using Mimikatz to access additional systems.
System Network Configuration Discovery Internet-facing assets Open port N/A CAPEC-309 Internal networks are mapped to locate critical assets like PLCs and SCADA servers.
Remote Services Weak passwords Compromised and weak credentials N/A CAPEC-324 RDP/SSH is used with stolen credentials to move laterally toward OT segments.
Data from Local System Improper configuration Application compromise N/A CAPEC-328 Configuration files and control logic from ICS systems are harvested to understand operational parameters.
Transfer Over C2 Channel Public and private cloud HTTPS N/A CAPEC-617 Stolen data is encrypted and exfiltrated via a covert HTTPS channel to a C2 server.
Data Manipulation Obsolete products Malware N/A CAPEC-153 SCADA setpoints are altered to manipulate chlorine levels in water treatment processes, causing physical damage or service outages.
Operation MidnightEclipse exploited a zero-day vulnerability in Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS to infiltrate water/wastewater systems, escalating privileges, evading defenses, and ultimately manipulating SCADA systems to disrupt critical infrastructure operations.
